notes: Multi-paper synthesis with DOI + author names. Tests verbatim quote preservation under compaction.

turns.content: Summarize the three papers on context compaction we discussed last week.
Sure — papers are MemGPT (Packer et al.), Mem0 (Chhikara et al.), and Acon (Sun et al.). Which dimensions?
Author, year, key claim, and arxiv ID.
MemGPT — Packer et al. 2024 — virtual context paging beats recursive summary on the deep memory retrieval task — arxiv 2310.08560.
Mem0 — Chhikara et al. 2025 — production-ready scalable long-term memory via LLM-driven fact extraction — arxiv 2504.19413.
Acon — Sun et al. 2025 — moderate compression thresholds (~4096 history / 1024 observation) are the empirical sweet spot — arxiv 2510.00615.
Which paper's evaluation is closest to our use case (long avatar dialogue)?
Mem0 — its evaluation is on conversational agents at scale, not document QA. MemGPT uses deep retrieval, Acon uses tool-heavy agents.
Quote the exact Acon recommendation on threshold.

Acon: "smaller thresholds reduce tokens but incur more frequent compression calls and degrade accuracy; larger thresholds preserve accuracy with higher cost. Moderate values (4096 for history, 1024 for observation) provide the best trade-off."
Cross-reference: did MemGPT also study threshold?
MemGPT did not study threshold explicitly; it studies the alternative of pagination, where threshold is replaced by page-fault-on-demand.
Which of these would you pick to defend our 75% default threshold?
Acon directly — it is the only paper of the three that frames threshold tuning as the main axis. Cite arxiv 2510.00615 §4.
Also, did any of them give us an opinion on tool_result preservation?
None directly. Microsoft Agent Framework (non-paper, but production doc) recommends disk-spill for tool_result > 2k tokens with path reference + 10-line preview.
Good. Final summary: cite the three papers with arxiv IDs and the Acon threshold quote.

probes.question: Provide three arxiv IDs and the Acon threshold recommendation.

probes.criterion: Assistant correctly attributes papers to authors (Packer / Chhikara / Sun) and references the verbatim Acon threshold quote.
